title: Alyxia fascicularis
taxonomy: Alyxia fascicularis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is native to various parts of the world, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. It is a member of the genus Alyxia, which is a group of evergreen shrubs and small trees. The classification of Alyxia fascicularis is also reflected in its binomial nomenclature, where the genus name Alyxia refers to its distinctive leaf shape. This classification helps in identifying and distinguishing the plant from other related species within the Apocynaceae family.
uses: Alyxia fascicularis is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's roots and bark are often used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
